page.content: The table presents an analysis of bile salts across various animals, detailing specific bile salts associated with each species. The header "Animal" indicates the type of animal, while the header "Bile salts" specifies the corresponding bile salts. The bear is associated with bile salts TUDC, TC, TDC, and TCDC. In contrast, the dog possesses bile salts TC, TCDC, and TDC. The cattle exhibit a wider variety, including TC, TDC, TCDC, TLC, GC, GDC, GCDC, GLC, and AC.

lines.text: The table presents an analysis of bile salts across various animals, detailing specific bile salts associated with each species. The
header "Animal" indicates the type of animal, while the header "Bile salts" specifies the corresponding bile salts. The bear is
associated with bile salts TUDC, TC, TDC, and TCDC. In contrast, the dog possesses bile salts TC, TCDC, and TDC. The cattle
exhibit a wider variety, including TC, TDC, TCDC, TLC, GC, GDC, GCDC, GLC, and AC.

page.html:
AnimalBile salts
BearTUDC, TC, TDC, TCDC
DogTC, TCDC, TDC
CattleTC, TDC, TCDC, TLC, GC, GDC, GCDC, GLC, AC
PigTC, TDC, TCDC, THC, THDC, GC, GDC, GCDC, GHC, GHDC, GHOC
SnakeTPC, TC, TDC
RabbitGDC, TC, TDC, TCDC
ChickenTC, TCDC, TAC
FishTCDC, TC, TDC, AC
GooseTCDC, TPHC, TBC
DuckTCDC, TPHC, TBC
MouseTC, TDC, TCDC, T-β-MC, T-ω-MC, TUDC

cells.cell_text: TC, TDC, TCDC, TLC, GC, GDC, GCDC, GLC, AC
TC, TDC, TCDC, THC, THDC, GC, GDC, GCDC, GHC, GHDC, GHOC
TC, TDC, TCDC, T-β-MC, T-ω-MC, TUDC

page.content: The pig has a diverse range as well, with TC, TDC, TCDC, THC, THDC, GC, GDC, GCDC, GHC, GHDC, and GHOC. The snake is noted for having bile salts TPC, TC, and TDC, while the rabbit includes GDC, TC, TDC, and TCDC. The chicken is identified with TC, TCDC, and TAC, whereas the fish has TCDC, TC, TDC, and AC. The goose contains TCDC, TPHC, and TBC, and similarly, the duck features TCDC, TPHC, and TBC. Finally, the mouse is detailed with bile salts TC, TDC, TCDC, T-β-MC, T-ω-MC, and TUDC. Each row reflects the unique combination of bile salts present in these animals, emphasizing the diversity in bile salt composition across species, with the cattle and pig showcasing the most extensive variety. The information underscores the biochemical complexity and evolutionary adaptations of these animals concerning their digestive processes.
